key_insights.insight: Most psychiatric illnesses stem from brain health issues rather than mental illnesses, which shifts the focus to physical well-being.
key_insights.quote: Most psychiatric illnesses are not mental illnesses. They're brain health issues. When you reimagine mental health as brain health, it changes everything.

key_insights.insight: Depression, like chest pain, is a symptom and not a diagnosis; identifying the root cause is crucial for effective treatment.
key_insights.quote: Depression is like chest pain. It doesn't tell you what causes it, and it doesn't tell you what to do for it.

key_insights.insight: Mild traumatic brain injury is a significant, often overlooked, cause of psychiatric problems.
key_insights.quote: Mild traumatic brain injury is a major cause of psychiatric problems. And nobody knows about it because they don't look at the brain.

key_insights.insight: Each decision point in your day offers an opportunity to either support or harm your brain health.
key_insights.quote: Whenever you come to a decision point in your day... you ask yourself this question, takes three seconds. Is this good for my brain or bad for it?

key_insights.insight: Long-term restriction of blood flow to the brain through substances like caffeine and marijuana can harm brain development.
key_insights.quote: Long-term restriction of blood flow to the brain through these things you've described caffeine marijuana all of these things has a detrimental impact on the development of the brain

key_insights.insight: Genes load the gun, but lifestyle choices pull the trigger when it comes to diseases like obesity and heart disease.
key_insights.quote: Genes load the gun. It's what happens to us and what we choose to do that pulls the trigger.

key_insights.insight: Teenagers' brains are particularly vulnerable to damage from poor nutrition, drugs, and alcohol due to ongoing myelination.
key_insights.quote: From the time you're 15 to 25, it's gone through wild construction... This masterpiece building, if you will, is under construction until you're 25. So many teenagers, it's the crappy food. It's just like throwing poison into the construction zone.

key_insights.insight: Forgiveness, facilitated by understanding another person's perspective, is crucial for personal well-being and brain health.
key_insights.quote: When you're holding on to that toxicity, it's sort of like drinking poison and expecting the other person to die.

key_insights.insight: Low levels of Vitamin D are linked to a range of negative health outcomes, including a smaller brain.
key_insights.quote: People who have low vitamin d levels are much more likely to die from covid... low vitamin d which occurs in about 60 of the population is associated virtually with every bad thing including a smaller brain

actionable_advice.advice: Ask yourself, 'Is this good for my brain?' at every decision point.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pause for three seconds before making a choice and consider the impact on your brain health, factoring in self-love and personal goals.

actionable_advice.advice: Monitor your fasting blood sugar levels and aim for under 90.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use a glucose monitor or consult with a healthcare professional to track blood sugar and consider dietary changes or medication if needed.

actionable_advice.advice: Check the ingredients of your personal products to avoid hormone disruptors.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use apps like "Think Dirty" or scan the EWG database to assess the toxicity of products like toothpaste, deodorant, and shampoo.

actionable_advice.advice: Get your Vitamin D levels checked and supplement if needed.
actionable_advice.how_to: Ask your doctor for a Vitamin D blood test. If deficient, supplement with Vitamin D3 and K2, considering your skin tone and sun exposure.
Establish a relaxing bedtime ritual, ensure your room is dark, quiet, and cool, and avoid caffeine and large meals before bed.

actionable_advice.advice: Write out what you want in various parts of your life.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take a blank page and outline what you want in your relationships, your work, in your money, in your physical, emotional, and spiritual health to activate your prefrontal cortex.

memorable_stories: Dr. Amen's story of his dad changing after he started taking care of his brain, especially his dad finally saying that he was proud of him
Story of Dr. Amen's nephew who had a cyst in his left temporal lobe and his violent behavior stopped after neurosurgery
contrarian_claims: Most psychiatric illnesses are not mental illnesses, they're brain health issues.
The mental health industry is, in some ways, destroying mental health.
Caffeine, though widely used, has a significant cost, including reducing blood flow in the brain
Alcohol is not a health food, and marijuana is not innocuous.
Penis envy is not the root cause of people's problems; rather, it's brain envy.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Doctor & Therapist To The Worlds Superstars: Justin Bieber, Miley Cyrus & Bella Hadid!: Dr Amen
